# Initialize Inconvo Project with PostgreSQL

## Background
Inconvo requires a database connection to introspect the schema and generate the semantic model. Your task is to initialize a new Inconvo project and configure it to connect to an existing PostgreSQL database.

## Requirements
- You must initialize an Inconvo project in `/home/user/myproject`.
- You must configure the database connection by creating a `.env` file in the project directory.
- The `.env` file must contain the `DATABASE_URL` environment variable set to `postgresql://inconvo:inconvo@localhost:5432/inconvo_db`.
- The project must have a valid `inconvo.yaml` file after initialization.

## Implementation Guide
1. Navigate to `/home/user/myproject`.
2. Create a `.env` file and set `DATABASE_URL=postgresql://inconvo:inconvo@localhost:5432/inconvo_db`.
3. Run the appropriate command to initialize the Inconvo project (e.g., `npx inconvo@latest init` or the equivalent if it's a manual initialization).

## Constraints
- Project path: `/home/user/myproject`
- Database URL: `postgresql://inconvo:inconvo@localhost:5432/inconvo_db`

This rails guide seems to indicate that database.yml is optional and indeed I can run a server with `rails s` even with no database.yml present.
...
For me, I use a `.env` file to set environment variables.
I had to run my command with `foreman start` which sources the env before the process gets run
``` $ rm config/database.yml $ foreman run bundle exec rails runner 'puts ENV["DATABASE_URL"].present?'
...
You can definitely run rails without a `database.yml`.|

export DATABASE_URL="postgresql://user:password@localhost/database"

# Documentation of DB Connection environment variable is inconsistent · Issue #678 · boxyhq/saas-starter-kit
created: 2023-10-21, last updated: 2026-04-17, [Read more](https://github.com/boxyhq/saas-starter-kit/issues/678)

In the `/.env.example` file there is a variable named `DATABASE_URL` that is also present in the following files in this boilerplate:
- `app.json`
- `README.md`
- `deploy.template.yaml`
- `env.ts`
...
@ontoneio SAML Jackson uses `DB_URL` as the environment variable name.
The `DATABASE_URL` is used by the `@prisma/client` package.
Both are different packages hence the different variable names.
Updating `DB_URL` to `DATABASE_URL` will effectively break SAML Jackson.
...
@devkiran DB_URL is not used by the embedded npm, we pass the URL to it.
...
```
APP_URL=""
AUTH_PROVIDERS=""

created: 2020-10-10, last updated: 2026-05-10, [Read more](https://forum.storj.io/t/cant-set-up-config-yaml-before-a-new-node-starts/9635)

Please, use the `storagenode setup` instead.
...
The `storagenode run` will not create them to prevent the start from scratch if there is something missed - either `config.yaml` or databases or folders tree (this useful when your disk accidentally unmounted for example).
...
We do not require to create a `config.yaml` in advance as you can see.
...
Please make sure that you explicitly mention that not even `config.yaml` can be there, this will be very helpful for sysadmins used to usual conventions regarding service configuration.
...
Update: as turned out, we actually can have a different folders in the storage destination, but you should have an empty `storage` subfolder (or do not have at all) and no files in the folder, especially `config.yaml`.
So, reformulated this way:
> If you setup a brand new node, make sure that destination folder for storage doesn’t have a `config.yaml` or/and non-empty `storage` folder inside, otherwise the storagenode container could fail to start.
...
Depends on the system, probably.
...
In other words, the existence of a configuration file is a flag for the service being configured or not, not for the service being already deployed.

name: inconvo-oss

services:
  dev-server:
    image: ghcr.io/inconvoai/inconvo/dev-server:${INCONVO_VERSION:-latest}
    ports:
      - "26686:26686"
    extra_hosts:
      - "host.docker.internal:host-gateway"
    volumes:
      - ${INCONVO_DATA_DIR:?INCONVO_DATA_DIR is required}:/data
      - ${INCONVO_CREDENTIALS_DIR:?INCONVO_CREDENTIALS_DIR is required}:/inconvo/credentials:ro
    environment:
      # Database path inside container
      - INCONVO_LOCAL_DB_PATH=/data/inconvo.db
      # Internal communication with sandbox (runs on host)
      - INCONVO_SANDBOX_BASE_URL=${INCONVO_SANDBOX_BASE_URL:-http://host.docker.internal:8787}
      - INCONVO_SANDBOX_API_KEY=${INCONVO_SANDBOX_API_KEY:?INCONVO_SANDBOX_API_KEY is required}
      # Skip env validation (validated at CLI level)
      - SKIP_ENV_VALIDATION=true
      # User configuration (passed from CLI via ~/.inconvo/config.env)
      - OPENAI_API_KEY=${OPENAI_API_KEY:-}
      - DATABASE_DIALECT=${DATABASE_DIALECT:-}
      - INCONVO_DATABASE_URL=${INCONVO_DATABASE_URL:-}
      - INCONVO_DATABASE_SCHEMA=${INCONVO_DATABASE_SCHEMA:-}
      - INCONVO_BIGQUERY_PROJECT_ID=${INCONVO_BIGQUERY_PROJECT_ID:-}
      - INCONVO_BIGQUERY_DATASET=${INCONVO_BIGQUERY_DATASET:-}
      - INCONVO_BIGQUERY_LOCATION=${INCONVO_BIGQUERY_LOCATION:-}
      - INCONVO_BIGQUERY_KEYFILE=${INCONVO_BIGQUERY_KEYFILE:-}
      - INCONVO_BIGQUERY_CREDENTIALS_JSON=${INCONVO_BIGQUERY_CREDENTIALS_JSON:-}
      - INCONVO_BIGQUERY_CREDENTIALS_BASE64=${INCONVO_BIGQUERY_CREDENTIALS_BASE64:-}
      - INCONVO_BIGQUERY_MAX_BYTES_BILLED=${INCONVO_BIGQUERY_MAX_BYTES_BILLED:-}
      - INCONVO_SECRET_KEY=${INCONVO_SECRET_KEY:-}
      # Telemetry (set to true to disable)
      - DISABLE_TELEMETRY=${DISABLE_TELEMETRY:-false}
      - NEXT_PUBLIC_DISABLE_TELEMETRY=${DISABLE_TELEMETRY:-false}
      # Demo mode
      - USE_DEMO_DATABASE=${USE_DEMO_DATABASE:-false}
    depends_on:
      demo-db:
        condition: service_healthy
        required: false
    restart: unless-stopped

  demo-db:
    image: postgres:16-alpine
    profiles:
      - demo
    ports:
      - "26687:5432"
    environment:
      - POSTGRES_USER=inconvo
      - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=inconvo
      - POSTGRES_DB=demo
    volumes:
      - demo-db-data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
      - ${INCONVO_INIT_SCRIPT:-./demo-db-init.sql}:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/init.sql:ro
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U inconvo -d demo"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  demo-db-data:
    name: inconvo-demo-db-data

# Understanding the PostgreSQL Connection String Format
created: 2024-12-23, last updated: 2026-03-16, [Read more](https://www.w3resource.com/PostgreSQL/snippets/understanding-the-postgresql-connection-string-format.php)

A PostgreSQL connection string, also known as a connection URL, is a format used to specify connection parameters to connect to a PostgreSQL database.
The URL provides details like the host, port, database name, user, and password.
This format is widely used in applications for easily configuring database access.
Format of the PostgreSQL Connection String:
The general format for a PostgreSQL connection string is:
```
postgresql://[user[:password]@][host][:port][/dbname][?options]
```
Explanation:
- **user:** Username used to connect to the database.
- **password: ** Password for the specified user (optional but recommended).
- **host:** Database server hostname (e.g., localhost or an IP address).
- **port:** Port on which the PostgreSQL server listens (default is 5432).
- **dbname: ** Name of the database you are connecting to.
- **options:** Optional parameters for additional configurations (e.g., SSL mode, application name).
...

2019-09-04
```
What happens is that the first comamnd line argument is used by `psql` as
the dbname here, and the connection string parsing provided by `libpq` is
done on that parameter.
So that for the `-d dbname` parameter, you can
actually pass in a whole connection string:
```
$ psql -d "host=localhost port=5432 dbname=taop user=taop"
psql (12devel, server 10.10)
...
```
